findings.title: Writeback authority is only enforced when an active loop lease exists
findings.rationale: The authority guard used by capture-review-truth-snapshot and record-review-outcome returns Ok when no loop lease exists, when the lease has no verifier, or when the lease belongs to a different mission. That means the runtime can still mint a writeback token and accept parent-looking record-review-outcome calls outside the active-lease path, while the spec requires reviewer lanes not to mint usable review_truth_snapshot authority or terminalize by spoofing parent-review-loop. Existing tests also exercise successful parent writeback without first creating a verifier-backed lease, so this bypass is part of the accepted behavior rather than an untested edge only.
findings.suggested_next_action: Make parent writeback authority mandatory for review truth capture/outcome regardless of lease absence, or add an explicit non-reviewer parent capability/caller context that reviewer lanes cannot mint; then add a regression where a child lane with no active lease cannot capture truth or record a clean outcome by setting reviewer to parent-review-loop.

findings.title: A single generic legacy reviewer id can satisfy all required review lanes
findings.rationale: The lane-completion guard derives required code_bug_correctness and spec_intent_or_proof lanes for clean correctness reviews, but reviewer_output_is_legacy_combined_lane returns true for any reviewer id beginning with review_ or review- that lacks the lane tokens. Consequently one durable output such as review_token_lifecycle can satisfy both required lanes for a code-producing correctness bundle, which weakens the machine-enforced guarantee that clean code review has distinct required lane/profile coverage.
findings.suggested_next_action: Remove the broad legacy-combined bypass for new bundles, or gate it to explicitly marked historical artifacts; add a regression proving a single generic review_* output cannot clear a code-producing correctness bundle that requires both code/correctness and spec/intent or proof coverage.
